report_name: Ernst & Young Discloses Data Breach via Third-Party Support System

threat_actors.name: Unnamed Actor
threat_actors.description: The unauthorized third party responsible for the supply chain attack against Ernst & Young's ITSM platform, accessing and exfiltrating sensitive client tax data between March and April 2026.
threat_actors.goals: Exfiltration of sensitive personal and financial data from EY's clients for potential fraud or identity theft.

attack_patterns.name: Trusted Relationship
attack_patterns.description: The attacker exploited the trusted relationship between Ernst & Young and its third-party IT service management (ITSM) platform to gain initial access to sensitive data.

attack_patterns.name: Data from Cloud Storage
attack_patterns.description: The unauthorized actor accessed and downloaded sensitive documents attached to IT support tickets stored within the compromised third-party cloud-based ITSM platform.

identities.name: Ernst & Young
identities.description: Global professional services and accounting firm that was the victim of a data breach via a compromised third-party IT service management platform used for its tax practice.

infrastructure.name: Third-Party ITSM Platform
infrastructure.description: A third-party Information Technology Service Management (ITSM) platform used by EY's tax practice, which was compromised by an attacker to access and exfiltrate sensitive client documents.
